pre_commit_gate.cmd: npm test -- test/cache/cache.test.ts test/search/search.test.ts test/mcp/tools.test.ts test/graph/graph.test.ts test/write/apply.test.ts test/cli/read-commands.test.ts test/cli/doctor.test.ts
npm test -- test/cache/cache.test.ts test/search/search.test.ts test/mcp/tools.test.ts test/graph/graph.test.ts test/write/apply.test.ts test/cli/read-commands.test.ts test/cli/doctor.test.ts

x-srs-remediation-scope.mode: remediate latest SRS verification findings only

deferred.reason: Performance remediation is out of scope for this plan.
Performance remediation is out of scope for this plan.
Performance remediation is out of scope for this plan.

phases.title: Cache source-of-truth hardening
phases.goal: Prevent cache-only facts from being returned as public read results.

tasks.title: Add source-authenticated search result hydration
method_signatures: export function rehydrateSearchResultsFromSource(result: SearchResultSet, sourceDocuments: readonly SearchDocument[]): { result: SearchResultSet; mismatchCount: number }
async function loadSearchSourceAudit(root: WorkspaceRoot): Promise<readonly SearchDocument[]>
export function searchWorkspaceFromReadModel(input: SearchInput, model: ReadModel, options?: { extraWarnings?: Diagnostic[]; sourceDocuments?: readonly SearchDocument[] }): SearchResultSet
implementation_guide: Build a source-derived audit document set in searchWorkspace when cached search results are used.
Keep searchWorkspaceFromReadModel synchronous by passing already loaded sourceDocuments through options.
Remove cache search results whose entity key is absent from source YAML.
Rehydrate public fields such as title, scope, path, and documentId from source documents when the entity exists.
Drop results whose matchedFields cannot be observed in source text for the query.
Emit SEARCH_CACHE_SOURCE_MISMATCH when one or more cache-only or field-mismatched results are removed or rehydrated.
Keep BM25 scoring and latency optimization out of this task.
tasks.rationale: A cache and manifest pair can be tampered together; source-derived rehydration blocks YAML-absent public results and source-inconsistent public DTO fields.
pitfalls: Do not change cacheMode bypass behavior.
Do not rebuild the search index as part of this task.
Do not treat key existence alone as source authentication.
tasks.test_guidance: success: normal cache hit returns source-equivalent id/title/scope/path/matchedFields; failure: fake cache-only ID is filtered with SEARCH_CACHE_SOURCE_MISMATCH; boundary: same ID with tampered title or scope is rehydrated from source and warns.
dod: YAML-absent cache search results are not returned.
Source-inconsistent public fields are rehydrated or dropped.

rollback.command: 1) Revert edits in search/read-model files. 2) Run npm test -- test/cache/cache.test.ts test/search/search.test.ts.

tasks.title: Make entity cache hits source-verified before returning requirements
method_signatures: async function confirmCachedRequirementAgainstSource(root: WorkspaceRoot, cached: RegisteredRequirement): Promise<boolean>
async function getRequirementFromEntityCache(input: GetRequirementInput, root: WorkspaceRoot): Promise<{ result?: RequirementResult; warnings: Diagnostic[] }>
implementation_guide: Load the source YAML document for the cached requirement path.
Compare key requirement fields and relation targets before returning cache DTO.
Treat mismatch as cache miss and return source-derived data with ENTITY_CACHE_SOURCE_MISMATCH warning.
tasks.rationale: Exact lookup must never return cache-only requirement facts.
pitfalls: Do not fold this task into performance optimization.
Do not remove the existing cache hash freshness checks.
tasks.test_guidance: Tamper requirement shard and manifest together, then assert YAML source statement and relations are returned.
dod: Exact cache hit is returned only after source confirmation.

rollback.command: 1) Revert edits in src/core/requirements.ts and src/core/read-model.ts. 2) Run npm test -- test/cache/cache.test.ts.

phases.title: MCP schema and output contract hardening
phases.goal: Make MCP invalid params and output schema behavior match SRS.

tasks.title: Enforce apply change XOR in MCP input schema
method_signatures: export const applyChangeInputSchema = z.strictObject(...).superRefine((value, ctx) => void)
async function parseToolInput(tool: RegisteredTool, input: unknown, toolName: string): Promise<unknown>
implementation_guide: Add zod superRefine count check for proposalId/proposalPath/change.
Keep Core hasExactlyOneChangeSource guard.
tasks.rationale: Shape errors must be JSON-RPC invalid params, not Core domain failures.
pitfalls: Treat property absence and undefined as no source.
tasks.test_guidance: Invalid zero-source and two-source inputs fail before handler execution.

rollback.command: 1) Revert edits in src/mcp/schemas.ts and tests. 2) Run npm test -- test/mcp/tools.test.ts.

tasks.title: Replace generic MCP output schema with tool-specific DTO schemas
method_signatures: export function toolOutputSchemaFor(name: string): z.ZodTypeAny
export const searchOutputSchema: z.ZodTypeAny
export const graphOutputSchema: z.ZodTypeAny
export const applyOutputSchema: z.ZodTypeAny
implementation_guide: Define common result base schema.
Return tool-specific schema by tool name.
Validate error structuredContent as well as success structuredContent.
tasks.rationale: MCP metadata must describe the Core DTO shape instead of only requiring ok.
pitfalls: Do not duplicate every internal DTO field.
tasks.test_guidance: Assert output schema required fields for search, graph, apply, and error results.
dod: Output schemas describe core public fields and validate error outputs.

rollback.command: 1) Revert edits in src/mcp structured-content/tools/schemas files. 2) Run npm test -- test/mcp/tools.test.ts.

tasks.title: Add MCP success path black-box coverage
method_signatures: async function callRegisteredTool(server: McpServer, name: string, args: unknown): Promise<CallToolResult>
implementation_guide: Call registered MCP handlers through schema parse and output validation.
Cover read_document, preview_requirement_id, trace_requirement, propose_change, and apply_change success paths.
tasks.rationale: Tool registration tests do not prove success path behavior.
pitfalls: Run propose tests in temp workspaces only.
tasks.test_guidance: success: read_document, preview_requirement_id, trace_requirement, propose_change, and apply_change return expected DTO fields; failure: apply uses allowApply=false or invalid params negative path; boundary: propose then apply in one temp workspace and verify source hash changes only after apply.
dod: Five MCP tools have adapter-level success tests.

rollback.command: 1) Remove new MCP success path tests. 2) Run npm test -- test/mcp/tools.test.ts.

phases.title: Graph diagnostics preservation
phases.goal: Preserve validation diagnostics through graph, trace, and impact results.

tasks.title: Preserve workspace diagnostics in GraphResult
method_signatures: export function buildGraph(workspace: LoadedWorkspace, graphType?: GraphType): GraphResult
export function buildGraphFromRegistry(registry: RequirementRegistry, graphType?: GraphType, diagnostics?: DiagnosticBag): GraphResult
buildGraph(graphType?: GraphType): GraphResult
implementation_guide: Pass workspace.diagnostics into GraphResult.
Dedupe diagnostics deterministically.
Keep graph node and edge sorting stable.
tasks.rationale: Invalid relation targets must not disappear in graph output.
pitfalls: Do not change traversal semantics in this task.
tasks.test_guidance: success: valid graph JSON keeps existing deterministic nodes and edges with errorCount 0; failure: unknown relation target appears in validate and graph JSON diagnostics; boundary: graph type filtering preserves diagnostics summary and sorting.
dod: Graph JSON preserves invalid relation diagnostics.

rollback.command: 1) Revert graph diagnostics edits. 2) Run npm test -- test/graph/graph.test.ts.

tasks.title: Propagate graph diagnostics through trace and impact
method_signatures: export function traceRequirement(input: TraceRequirementInput, graph: GraphResult): TraceResult
export function impactRequirement(input: ImpactInput, graph: GraphResult): ImpactResult
implementation_guide: Merge graph diagnostics into trace and impact results.
Add duplicates and conflicts_with behavior tests.
tasks.rationale: Trace and impact are public graph consumers and must preserve graph diagnostics.
pitfalls: Do not modify the impact relation matrix beyond test coverage.
tasks.test_guidance: Invalid relation target diagnostics remain visible in trace and impact.
dod: Trace and impact preserve graph diagnostics.

rollback.command: 1) Revert trace and impact diagnostics edits. 2) Run npm test -- test/graph/graph.test.ts test/mcp/tools.test.ts.

phases.title: Write proposal validation and user recovery
phases.goal: Reject malformed stored proposals deterministically and provide recovery guidance.

tasks.title: Validate stored proposal patch paths before apply
method_signatures: export async function readProposalAt(root: WorkspaceRoot, storePath: StorePath): Promise<ProposalDocument>
export function buildPatchOperations(change: ProposedChange): JsonPatchOperation[]
implementation_guide: Add JSON Pointer pattern to proposal schema.
Run buildPatchOperations for stored proposals before apply.
Return deterministic patch/proposal validation code.
tasks.rationale: Externally written proposal YAML must be validated before it reaches mutation logic.
pitfalls: Keep semantic array bounds checks in code rather than schema.
tasks.test_guidance: Slashless and URI-fragment pointers are rejected without source YAML mutation.
dod: Malformed stored proposal patches fail before source mutation.

rollback.command: 1) Revert proposal schema and write path edits. 2) Run npm test -- test/write/apply.test.ts test/mcp/tools.test.ts.

tasks.title: Add recovery guidance to apply failures
method_signatures: function applyFailure(code: ApplyFailureCode | string, message: string, diagnostics?: DiagnosticBag): ApplyResult
function recoveryDetailsForApplyFailure(code: string): JsonObject
implementation_guide: Add recovery details by failure code.
Expose recovery in JSON diagnostics.
Keep existing error codes stable.
tasks.rationale: Users need deterministic next steps after apply rejection.
pitfalls: Do not include destructive VCS commands in recovery text.
tasks.test_guidance: Assert recovery details for stale, validation, policy, and lock failures.
dod: Major apply failures include recovery guidance.

rollback.command: 1) Revert apply recovery guidance edits. 2) Run npm test -- test/write/apply.test.ts test/cli/req-write.test.ts.

phases.title: CLI and SRS coverage tests
phases.goal: Close black-box coverage gaps for CLI, doctor, quiet option, and out-of-scope guarantees.

tasks.title: Add CLI black-box tests for init and cache commands
method_signatures: async function runCli(args: string[], options: { cwd?: string }): Promise<{ status: number; stdout: string; stderr: string }>
implementation_guide: Test init in temp workspace.
Test cache rebuild JSON output.
Test cache clean removes artifacts and keeps source YAML.
tasks.rationale: CLI SRS acceptance requires process-level evidence.
pitfalls: Do not mutate repository fixtures during CLI cache tests.
tasks.test_guidance: success: temp workspace init, cache rebuild JSON, and cache clean JSON return expected files and fields; failure: invalid root returns non-zero machine-readable error; boundary: no-cache graph/search/export/apply creates no new cache artifact or stale marker.
dod: init, cache rebuild, and cache clean have CLI black-box tests.

rollback.command: 1) Remove new CLI black-box tests. 2) Run npm test -- test/cli/read-commands.test.ts test/cache/cache.test.ts.

tasks.title: Strengthen doctor, quiet option, and OOS negative tests
method_signatures: export async function doctor(input: DoctorInput): Promise<DoctorResult>
function renderHumanResult(result: MachineResult): string
implementation_guide: Add doctor checks for MCP command availability and stdout policy.
Add quiet option output tests.
Add OOS negative smoke tests for HTTP, DB files, and daemon behavior.
tasks.rationale: Doctor and OOS requirements need observable release evidence.
tasks.test_guidance: success: doctor JSON includes MCP command availability and stdout/stderr policy check codes; failure: temp root without .speckiwi returns required-file diagnostics; boundary: quiet suppresses human success stdout while quiet JSON remains parseable; OOS: default command paths show no HTTP server, DB file creation, or daemon spawn.
dod: Doctor, quiet, and OOS requirements have observable tests.

rollback.command: 1) Revert doctor and related test edits. 2) Run npm test -- test/cli/doctor.test.ts test/hardening/security.test.ts test/cli/read-commands.test.ts.

risks.risk: Source-authenticated cache audit may increase search latency.
risks.mitigation: Keep 500ms performance remediation out of scope and track as separate performance plan.

risks.risk: Tool-specific output schemas may become brittle.
risks.mitigation: Require only public observable fields and allow passthrough extension.

risks.risk: Graph diagnostics preservation may affect CLI exit behavior.
risks.mitigation: Add explicit CLI tests before changing behavior.
